scope_change_note: Issue 002 expands coverage from 4 to 6 protocols. Compound (compound-v3) and Euler (euler-v2) added. MoM comparisons against Apr 30 use the 6-protocol baseline as recorded by the dashboard's own historical sector_snapshots row, not the 4-protocol baseline published in Issue 001.

compound-v3.name: Compound

captured_pre_fix_value.explanation: Original May 31 dashboard freeze. The card formula totalSupply = tvl + borrowed read DefiLlama's chainTvls.Ethereum and chainTvls.Ethereum-borrowed for Compound V3. For Aave V3 and Spark that formula is correct (tvl = unborrowed liquidity, adding borrowed reconstructs total supply). For Compound V3, DefiLlama prices some collateral assets off-market and the resulting sum overstated the protocol's Ethereum supply by roughly $340M vs on-chain Comet totals at the May 31 snapshot. The corrected values above replace these for §06.5 and subsequent issues. Pre-fix figures preserved for the audit trail, not for citation. See content/snapshots/2026-06-compound-card-fix.md for the patch detail.

compound-v3.correction_note: Compound V3 total_supply_usd / tvl_usd / borrows_usd / utilization_pct above have been restated to match on-chain Comet totals (USDC + USDT + WETH + USDS markets). All other fields (fees_7d_usd, revenue_30d_usd, net_deposits_30d_usd) are unchanged — they were sourced from DefiLlama fee endpoints, not from the buggy supply-card path.

euler-v2.name: Euler

captured_pre_fix_value.explanation: Original May 31 dashboard freeze. The card formula totalSupply = tvl + borrowed read DefiLlama's chainTvls.Ethereum and chainTvls.Ethereum-borrowed for Euler V2. Same pattern as Compound V3 (see compound-v3 entry): DefiLlama's protocol-level Ethereum adapter overstates the on-chain truth, in this case by approximately $70M on the supplied leg (entirely on the tvl/available leg; borrowed reconciles within $20M). Verified June 2026 against on-chain reads of 53 of 57 active EVK vaults (the 4 unmatched vaults total roughly $310K of TVL per DefiLlama and cannot account for the gap). The corrected values above reflect the on-chain truth at the time of audit. Pre-fix figures preserved for the audit trail, not for citation. See content/snapshots/2026-06-euler-card-check.md for the verification detail.

euler-v2.correction_note: Euler V2 total_supply_usd / tvl_usd / borrows_usd / utilization_pct above have been restated to match on-chain EVK vault totals (Σ totalAssets and Σ totalBorrows across active Ethereum vaults). All other fields (fees_7d_usd, revenue_30d_usd, net_deposits_30d_usd) are unchanged — they were sourced from DefiLlama fee endpoints, not from the buggy supply-card path. The §06.6 protocol-level header in early drafts cited $549M / $896M for May 31 / Apr 30; the corrected May 31 figure is $469M, which changes the MoM percentage from −38.7% to roughly −47.7% if Apr 30 was correctly captured. Re-audit Apr 30 before citing the new MoM number.

apr_30_onchain_baseline.explanation: On-chain re-read of Euler V2 EVK vaults at the Ethereum block closest to 2026-04-30T23:59 UTC, sourced via DefiLlama /pools (vault list) + Goldsky euler-v2-mainnet subgraph (vault addresses) + DefiLlama /coins/prices/historical (USD prices at the same timestamp). This baseline coexists with the dashboard-derived Apr 30 figure in content/snapshots/2026-04-30-baseline.json — the two are different methodologies and Issue 002's §06.6 protocol-level header should cite this on-chain reading for apples-to-apples MoM against the corrected May 31 figure. See content/snapshots/2026-06-euler-apr30-reaudit.md.

_sum_check.notes: Sum of per-protocol card supplies matches sector $33.06B (rounded)

per_protocol_detail._note: These readings were captured from the per-protocol detail pages on the dashboard. They may differ slightly from the per-protocol card values above due to a few minutes of price/data drift between page loads. For Compound and Euler the detail pages report substantially different totals from the cards; this is flagged below for reconciliation when writing the issue.

compound-v3.RECONCILE_NOTE: Detail page total $8.34B is materially higher than the Ethereum-only card value of $1.61B. The 49 markets count suggests the detail page is aggregating across all chains where Compound V3 is deployed (Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, etc.). The card is the Ethereum-only figure that feeds the sector aggregate. Confirm with check:upstream and the DefiLlama protocol/compound-v3 response before publishing.

euler-v2.RECONCILE_NOTE: Detail $447.70M is lower than card $539.39M. Could be a per-chain filter (Ethereum-only in detail vs Ethereum-only in card, but counted differently — Euler V2's EVK vault topology can lead to double-counting if shared collateral pools across vaults aren't deduped). 93 markets is plausible for Ethereum-only on Euler V2. Reconcile before publishing.

net_supply_flows_may.methodology_note: Net change in deposit base · constant prices · price moves excluded · DefiLlama token quantities
key_observations: LRT outflows (WEETH $844M, RSETH $221M, EZETH $64M, OSETH $46M) totaling ~$1.17B dominate the outflow side, consistent with the rsETH unpause / LRT-loop unwind narrative through May.
Aave V3 lost $503M net while Spark gained $752M and Morpho gained $759M — capital is rotating between Ethereum lending venues, not leaving the sector.

BTC-family inflows (CBBTC $479M, TBTC $153M, WBTC $113M, KBTC $73M) totaling ~$818M is the largest non-stable inflow group, reading as a rotation from ETH-collateral loops into BTC-collateral positions.
Stablecoin inflows (USDT $525M, USDS $475M, USDE $140M, USDC $115M, PYUSD $94M, RLUSD $74M) totaling ~$1.42B suggest fresh stablecoin supply rather than rotation.
Euler net -$338M is notable given its small absolute size; -$310M card net-deposits-30d confirms this as a real outflow, not a rounding artifact.

collateral_mix_may.note: ETH-family (WETH + WSTETH + WEETH) totals 38.6% of collateral. BTC-family (WBTC + CBBTC) totals 16.7%. Stablecoin collateral (USDT + USDC) totals 22.5%. The remaining 22.3% is the long tail.

borrow_mix_may.data_quirk_note: DefiLlama returns USDS borrows under DAI for some Spark markets; that share is folded into the DAI wedge here.

top_10_markets_supply.note: E-mode collateral, borrowing disabled for most users by design

Corrected 2026-06-07. Supply APY of 3.27% per content/snapshots/2026-05-rate-dispersion.json (the captured-data ground truth for May 31 USDC supply rates). Original hand-typed figures of 97.6% utilization, 9.62% supply APY, 10.95% borrow APY were never backed by captured data and were retracted at publication. Utilization and borrow APY remain unset because they were not captured. Aave V3 USDC was third-lowest of the six covered protocols on supply APY, not the squeeze leader.
SPK Farming Pool — 0% util by design (incentive program, not a borrow market)

open_data_gaps_for_issue_002: Curator HHI for Morpho on May 31 — pulled in earlier conversation as 3,103. Confirm against blue-api.morpho.org snapshot when running snapshot-curator-hhi.ts.
Top 3 Morpho curators (May 31): Sentora 38.6%, Steakhouse 34.1%, Gauntlet 21.2%, combined 93.9%. From prior dashboard pull. Reconcile with fresh GraphQL pull.
Liquidations 90D by protocol — needs liquidator_db read.
Oracle concentration — needs lib/oracles.ts roll-up against the May 31 sector snapshot.
Real Yield Spread time series for the month — confirm +1.54pp May 31 reading against FRED TB4WK and the blended stable APY.
Compound and Euler card-vs-detail reconciliation flagged above. Confirm Ethereum-only figures via DefiLlama /protocol/compound-v3 and /protocol/euler-v2.

Net supply flows by asset are captured per the Sankey but only the May (current month) view; the W/M/Q toggles weren't dumped. Pull weekly cuts via export-net-flow-daily.ts if granularity is needed in the writeup.
narrative_hooks_observed_in_data: LRT-loop unwind is the dominant May story on the outflow side. WEETH alone accounts for $844M of outflows. Concentrated in WETH supply on Aave V3 (post-WETH-LTV restoration May 17).
Spark and Morpho absorbed most of the rotated capital. Net +$752M and +$759M respectively. Aave V3 net -$503M.

BTC collateral rotation is the May counter-narrative. ~$818M of BTC-family inflows is the largest non-stable inflow group and reads as users moving from leveraged ETH loops to lower-correlation BTC positions.

Sector USDC supply APY dispersion is 330.5 bps at May 31, 1.33x the 12-month average, with Fluid at 5.93% leading and Euler V2 at 2.62% trailing. Aave V3 USDC sat at 3.27% supply APY, third-lowest of the six. USDT and WETH dispersion both ran below their 12-month baselines, leaving USDC as the only asset still elevated.

Sector utilization fell 2pp MoM despite borrows falling more in absolute terms than supply. Borrows down $1.33B, supply down $1.58B — supply contracted slightly more, but the utilization decline says the protocol-weighted denominator effect favored supply this month.

Real Yield Spread closed May at parity (-0.3 bps), up from -34 bps in April. The 33-bps tightening was the largest single-month compression of 2026 and the first month-end at parity since September 2025. The Treasury-bill leg held flat while the blended on-chain stable APY lifted 34 bps from 3.26% to 3.60% — a sector-wide rebalancing distributed across the four protocols in the blended-stable calculation, not concentrated in any single market.

Morpho idle ratio at 63.7% (vault capital sitting unallocated) is a structural metric worth flagging given the curator HHI context. Curators are holding capital back rather than deploying it into markets they don't trust.
